summary: High‑energy set with standout China>Rider and tight Crazy Fingers
blurb: The show delivers a vibrant opening with China Cat Sunflower into I Know You Rider and strong performances of Crazy Fingers and Ramble On Rose. Jerry’s lead work is textbook Dead, while the rhythm section stays tight throughout. The best soundboard capture showcases crisp audio and the band’s full‑throttle energy.
review:
On October 27 1990 the Grateful Dead took Paris’ Zenith by storm, rolling out a setlist that balanced classic firepower with fresh moments from their new keyboard duo. An electric opener of "Hell In A Bucket" gave way to a sparkling "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" that reviewers called “bright” and “high‑energy,” setting the tone for a night where Jerry’s lead guitar shone on "Sugaree," "Crazy Fingers," and "New Minglewood Blues." Phil’s bass was described as “amazing” and especially bouncy during the 6‑string venture on "Throwing Stones," while Bruce Hornsby’s honky‑tonk piano and Vince Welnick’s synth textures kept the harmonic canvas full. The second set built a soaring arc – from the melodic lift of "Ramble On Rose" through the expansive jam in "Playing In The Band" into a soulful "Stella Blue" that many fans cite as one of 1990’s tender highlights. Though a few recordings note occasional dips in energy and mixed‑quality sources, the best soundboard (gd90-10-27.sbd.braverman.9373.sbefail.shnf) captures the band tight, crisp, and full‑throttle, making this Paris gig a must‑listen for anyone tracking the Hornsby/Welnick era.

Overall, the performance is solidly in the upper‑mid tier of the 1990 European leg – not a legend‑status show, but certainly one that rewards repeated spins, especially on the recommended SBD source. The blend of classic Dead motifs with the new keyboard dynamic gives this night its unique flavor.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings (soundboard, audience DAT, UltraMatrix matrix) are clearly positive, praising the set's energy, standout songs and crisp sound; two negative/ mixed entries offer vague disappointment but lack specific performance criticism. The net effect is a solid positive sentiment, warranting a moderate +1.2 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard capture that highlights the band’s tight playing and brings out the full detail of Jerry’s lead work and the dual keyboards.

key_highlights: "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" segue delivers bright, high‑energy momentum.
"Crazy Fingers" showcases textbook Dead lead guitar from Jerry.
Second‑set climax with "Ramble On Rose," "Throwing Stones," and tender "Stella Blue" ranks among 1990’s top moments.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s lead guitar shines on Sugaree, Crazy Fingers and New Minglewood Blues; reviewers call his playing textbook Dead and especially on.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh delivers solid, sometimes "amazing" bass work; highlighted for a vibrant, bouncy synth‑bass adventure on Throwing Stones and a 6‑string contribution.
band_performance.Brent: Bruce Hornsby adds honky‑tonk piano flavor while Vince Welnick supplies synth textures; together they keep the sound full despite occasional mix balance issues for Vince.
band_performance.Mickey: Pre‑drum segment and overall percussion praised as tight; drum work considered solid throughout.
band_performance.Billy: Drums stay locked in, complementing the tight rhythm section and driving the high‑energy moments.
